what_it_does: Renders a design-system preview card showing status dots, task chips, pill/dropdown chips, a notification bell badge, and an avatar glyph with inline styling.
observations: Includes a dsCard metadata comment for Brand, named "Status dots, chips, bell", with a 700x260 viewport.
Loads _card.css and defines file-local CSS for the card, rows, labels, dots, chips, task chips, bell, badge, and avatar.
Uses six filled status dot colors: green, yellow, orange, red, blue, and neutral taupe.
Task chips pair colored dots with soft-fill backgrounds and short task labels.
Pill examples include a pending chip with dot, a dropdown-like task type chip, and a dark MENU chip.
Notification example uses an inline SVG bell with a red circular badge containing "7" and an avatar circle containing "◉".
standouts: The lead text explicitly states the design intent: filled status dots, soft-fill task chips, and a red notification badge without a plus sign.
Examples combine semantic labels with visible color swatches rather than showing color tokens separately.
The component preview is self-contained apart from shared card variables and base styling.
conventions: Uses CSS custom properties from the shared card stylesheet for typography, foreground/background colors, radii, and inverse colors.
Follows a repeated row pattern with a fixed-width uppercase label and flexible inline component examples.
Uses inline styles for demonstration-specific colors.
Uses compact, file-local class names tied to the preview content.
concerns: Hard-coded inline color values place palette decisions directly in markup.
The bell SVG and avatar glyph have no accessible names in this standalone markup.
Fixed label width may constrain longer labels or localization.
notes: This file is a compact visual reference for badge, dot, chip, bell, and avatar treatments.
questions: Are the six dot colors intended as the full status palette or just representative examples?
Should the bell and avatar examples include accessibility attributes in the preview markup?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it previews status dots, task chips, pills/dropdowns, and a notification badge.
apparent_themes: Soft fills paired with saturated markers
intention_signals: It seems intended to document how small status and notification UI elements should look, including their color usage, proportions, and label treatments.
novelty: The file combines several micro-components in one card and explicitly distinguishes a numeric red badge from a plus-style notification marker.
contribution: Contributes concrete badge, dot, chip, pill, dropdown-like chip, bell badge, and avatar visual treatments that a fused system could retain as small status and notification substrate examples.
summary_for_embedding: A standalone HTML design-system preview card demonstrating status dots, task chips, pill/dropdown chips, a red notification bell badge, and an avatar glyph with local CSS.
intention_for_embedding: This was for documenting compact status, task, menu, notification, and avatar badge treatments in a visual component ledger.
